llm_description: CSV table of regional archaeological radiocarbon dates for Mediterranean and Italian sites. Each row records region, site and context (layer/assemblage), cultural period, lab code and project code, uncalibrated radiocarbon age (date) with error, material and species dated, marine reservoir information (Marine? flag, ΔR, ΔR error, % marine), and calibrated BC ranges (1σ and 2σ). Latitude, longitude, notes, and bibliographic reference are included. Some fields are frequently blank (e.g., calibration ranges and reservoir parameters), so completeness varies by record.

CSV table from the data supplement to “A 3 million year index for North African humidity/aridity…”. Records Ocean Drilling Program Site 967 core/sample metadata (site, hole, core, section, interval, depths) with an age model (ky). Includes geochemical elemental concentrations (e.g., Al, Si, S, K, Ca, Ti, Mn, Fe, Rb, Sr, Zr, Ba), principal components (PC1, PC2), and derived proxies such as a rescaled ODP 967 dust proxy and a wet–dry index. The header and units appear partially noisy/duplicated, so some column naming may be uncertain.

CSV table from the data supplement to “A 3 million year index for North African humidity/aridity…”, containing Ocean Drilling Program (ODP) Site 967 sediment core measurements. Rows list core location fields (site, hole, core, section, interval, depths), age (ky), and elemental concentrations (e.g., Al, Si, Ca, Ti, Fe, Sr, Zr, Ba; ppm). It also includes principal components (PC1, PC2), a rescaled ODP 967 dust proxy based on magnetic hematite variations, and a derived wet-dry index. Column headers appear partially generic, suggesting some parsing/formatting noise.

CSV table from the data supplement to “A 3 million year index for North African humidity/aridity and the implication of potential pan-African Humid periods.” Records Ocean Drilling Program Site 967 core/sample metadata (hole, core, section, depths, age in kyr) with geochemical element concentrations (e.g., Al, Si, Ca, Fe, Sr, Ba) plus principal components (PC1, PC2), a rescaled dust proxy, and a wet–dry index. The header and units appear partially irregular in the sample, so some column labeling may require verification.

CSV table from the data supplement to “A 3 million year index for North African humidity/aridity…”. Records Ocean Drilling Program (ODP) Site 967 core/section intervals with depths, splice depth, and age (ky). Includes elemental concentrations (e.g., Al, Si, S, K, Ca, Ti, Mn, Fe, Rb, Sr, Zr, Ba; likely ppm), principal components (PC1, PC2), and derived climate indicators such as an ODP 967 dust proxy (magnetic hematite/aeolian dust flux) and a wet–dry index. The header is multi-row and somewhat noisy, so column alignment may require cleaning.

CSV table of Roman-era city/place records from the Kramabench Archeology Easy 3 (DataGov) benchmark. Each row lists an ancient toponym and its modern name, associated province and country, Barrington Atlas rank and reference, approximate start and end dates, longitude/latitude coordinates, and a short bibliography. Primary Key values (e.g., Hanson2016_*) uniquely identify records. Some fields are occasionally blank (notably End Date), indicating incomplete or uncertain coverage for certain places.

top_2_rows.Select Bibliography: BNP; Hansen 2006; Hansen and Nielsen 2004; PECS; Sear 2006.

llm_description: CSV table of ancient Roman-era city/place records from the Kramabench Archeology Easy 8 dataset. Each row links an ancient toponym to a modern toponym and lists province and country, Barrington Atlas rank and reference, approximate start and end dates, longitude/latitude coordinates, and selected bibliographic sources. The sample shows many entries for Greece and nearby regions with negative-year dates (BCE) and occasional missing end dates. Useful for historical gazetteer lookups, spatial analysis, and linking places to atlas references and literature.

top_2_rows.Select Bibliography: BNP; Hansen 2006; Hansen and Nielsen 2004; PECS; Sear 2006.

llm_description: CSV table of regional archaeological radiocarbon determinations, primarily Mediterranean sites (e.g., Sicily, Corsica, Italy). Fields include region, site and context, cultural period, laboratory code and project code, radiocarbon age and error, sample material/species, marine indicator and reservoir correction parameters (ΔR, % marine), calibrated BC ranges (1σ and 2σ), latitude/longitude, notes, and literature reference. Some entries have missing or inconsistent values (e.g., unknown lab codes or blank calibration fields), so completeness varies by record.

CSV table listing named conflicts with StartYear and EndYear, plus optional Fatalities. Additional derived time fields include Century and Decade (e.g., 900, 910, 920). The sample shows many European and Byzantine-related conflicts around the 10th century (e.g., Vikings, Magyars, Byzantium). Fatalities are often missing, with occasional numeric entries. Data appears to be an event index rather than detailed battle records; some conflict names include parentheses and bracketed regions, suggesting heterogeneous source naming.

CSV table listing Roman-era cities and settlements with a unique primary key, ancient and modern toponyms, associated province and country, Barrington Atlas rank and map reference, approximate start and end dates, longitude/latitude coordinates, and selected bibliography. Records appear sourced from scholarly and reference works (e.g., BNP, PECS, Hanson 2016). Coordinates are numeric and dates are often negative (BCE); some end dates and modern toponyms may be missing or listed as unknown. Suitable for historical GIS, toponym reconciliation, and spatial-temporal analysis of classical sites.

top_2_rows.Select Bibliography: BNP; Hansen 2006; Hansen and Nielsen 2004; PECS; Sear 2006.

llm_description: CSV table listing Roman-era city locations with unique primary keys and paired ancient and modern place names. Fields include province and country, Barrington Atlas rank and reference, approximate start and end dates, longitude/latitude coordinates, and selected bibliography sources. The sample shows many entries for Achaea (Greece/Albania) with negative years indicating BCE and some missing end dates. Useful for historical GIS, toponym reconciliation, and linking sites to atlas references and scholarly citations.

top_2_rows.Select Bibliography: BNP; Hansen 2006; Hansen and Nielsen 2004; PECS; Sear 2006.

llm_description: CSV table listing major world cities and related attributes. Columns include city names (original and ASCII), latitude/longitude, country, ISO2/ISO3 codes, first-level administrative region (admin_name), capital classification, population, and a city identifier. The sample shows large metropolitan areas worldwide (e.g., Tokyo, Delhi, Shanghai) with numeric populations and geocoordinates suitable for mapping, geocoding, or demographic analysis. Population values appear to be floating-point numbers and some fields may be blank (e.g., non-capital cities), so missing values should be handled during analysis.

CSV table of world cities with standardized names and geospatial attributes. Columns include city and ASCII name, latitude/longitude, country, ISO2/ISO3 codes, first-level admin_name, capital classification (e.g., primary/admin/minor), population, and a numeric city id. The sample shows major cities worldwide with large population figures, suitable for mapping, geocoding, and demographic comparisons. Population values appear as floats and some capital fields may be blank for non-capitals.

llm_description: CSV table from the Consumer Sentinel Network Data Book 2024 (Federal Trade Commission) summarizing identity theft reports by category and subtype. Rows include areas such as bank account, credit card, employment/tax-related, government documents/benefits, loans/leases, phone/utilities, and other identity theft, with counts of reports (e.g., “42,041”) and a percent change value (e.g., “0%”, “-25%”). Notes indicate consumers can report multiple types and that 14% of 2024 reports included more than one type. Some fields contain quoted text and commas, so parsing may require careful CSV handling.

Rankings are based on reports per 100,000 using 2023 U.S. Census population estimates. Examples of categories include Credit Bureaus and Information Furnishers, Identity Theft, Imposter Scams, and Online Shopping. Percentages and totals are included as footnotes in the file. The sample shows some extra header/blank columns (column_1–column_3), so column labeling may require cleanup.

llm_description: CSV table from the Federal Trade Commission’s Consumer Sentinel Network (CSN) Data Book 2024 summarizing consumer report volumes and breakdowns. It includes overall counts and shares by report type (Fraud, Identity Theft, Other), plus ranked lists such as the top 10 fraud categories with percent reporting monetary loss, total loss, and median loss; identity theft types by report count; and top 10 “Other” categories by report count. The file appears to contain multiple section headers and blank columns, so some fields may be noisy or require cleaning/parsing.

CSV table from the FTC Consumer Sentinel Network Data Book 2024 summarizing reports submitted by military consumers. It includes counts by report type (Fraud, Identity Theft, Other), fraud loss metrics (number with monetary loss, total and median loss), and ranked top 10 categories for fraud, identity theft types, and other complaint categories with associated report counts and, for fraud, percent reporting loss plus total and median losses. The file appears to be extracted from a formatted report, so column headers are generic and some values may require cleaning/reshaping.

llm_description: CSV table from the FTC Consumer Sentinel Network Data Book 2024 showing the top three identity theft types by year and number of reports. Rows list Theft Type, Year (2020–2024), and # of Reports, with values such as Bank Account, Credit Card, and Loan or Lease. The dataset supports comparing how report counts for these leading identity theft categories changed over the five-year period. Source text in the file cites the Federal Trade Commission; formatting includes some blank/header rows, so minor parsing cleanup may be needed.

Rows include a rank, area name, reports per 100,000 population (rate), and total number of reports; rankings are based on the per-capita rate. Notes indicate metro areas follow Office of Management and Budget definitions and population estimates use 2023 U.S. Census figures; state-specific data contributor reports are excluded. The file appears to have minimal headers and includes trailing footnote text, so column names may require inference.

llm_description: CSV table summarizing total wildfire-related helicopter requests by region from the Kramabench Wildfire Easy 13 dataset. It contains two fields: Region and Total Helicopter Requests. Regions include Alaska Area, Great Basin Area, Northwest Area, Northern Rockies Area, and others, with numeric totals per region (including some zeros such as Canada and Other). Suitable for comparing helicopter demand across coordination areas. The sample appears well-structured and clean, though only aggregated totals are provided (no dates or incident-level detail).

CSV table of monthly NOAA wildfire summary statistics. Each row represents a month (Date formatted as YYYYMM) with total Acres Burned, Number of Fires, and computed Acres Burned per Fire. The sample spans 200001 through 202503, enabling analysis of seasonal patterns, long-term trends, and changes in average fire size over time. Values appear to be numeric aggregates rather than event-level fire records.

CSV data dictionary for the Kramabench “wildfire easy 15” NOAA wildfires table. It maps each Variable Name to the corresponding label from Young et al. (2020), Table 1, plus an alternative plain-language description. Variables cover incident identifiers and geography (region, state, incident name, latitude/longitude), discovery/control dates and durations, weather and fuel metrics (humidity, wind, precipitation, ERC), suppression strategy indicators, causes, threatened structures, injuries/fatalities, fire size (hectares, >100 acres), and regional/yearly fire counts. Descriptions appear complete, though some fields have blank Table 1 entries.

llm_description: CSV table listing U.S. states and their total number of wildfire fires. It contains two columns: State and Total Fires, with one row per state (e.g., California, Texas, North Carolina). The data appears to be aggregated totals rather than time-series records, suitable for ranking or comparing wildfire incidence across states. Source context indicates it is part of the Kramabench Wildfire Hard 10 dataset (pages 45–54).

CSV table listing 2020 U.S. presidential election vote totals by state for Democratic (DEM) and Republican (REP) candidates. Includes state name, duplicated state field (usa_state), two-letter state code, and percent_democrat (Democratic share of the two-party total). Covers all states plus the District of Columbia. Values appear to be aggregated statewide counts; exact source and whether third-party votes are excluded is not specified.

CSV table listing U.S. states and territories with total acres burned by wildfire and population. Columns are State, Total Acres Burned, and Population. Most rows contain numeric acreage and population values; some entries have missing acres burned (e.g., District of Columbia, Puerto Rico). Useful for comparing wildfire burn area across states and relating it to population size. Source context suggests it is part of the Kramabench Wildfire (hard-10) dataset.

CSV table listing U.S. states/territories with total acres burned by wildfires and population. Columns include State, Total Acres Burned, and Population, enabling comparisons of wildfire impact across states and per-capita analysis. Some jurisdictions (e.g., District of Columbia, Puerto Rico) have missing acres-burned values, so totals may be incomplete for those rows. Data appears state-level and aggregated rather than event-level; the specific year or time range is not provided in the sample.

CSV table of wildfire incident records, primarily for U.S. regions including California and the Great Basin, spanning roughly 2002–2016. Columns include incident identifiers (region, state, incident_number, name), cause, start/controlled dates and duration, latitude/longitude, derived day-of-year fields, and fire size indicators (e.g., gt_100). It also includes weather/fuel metrics (humidity, wind, ERC, rainfall) and response strategy fields (dominant strategy percentiles, subdominant strategy, strategy indicators). Impact fields summarize threatened structures and reported injuries/fatalities. Some rows show inconsistent formatting and placeholder values, so a small amount of noise is likely.

CSV data dictionary for the Kramabench NOAA wildfires dataset. It lists each variable name alongside the corresponding description from Young et al. (2020, Table 1) and an alternative description. Variables cover incident identifiers (region, state, incident number, name), dates and timing (start/control dates, year, day-of-year, duration), location (latitude/longitude), weather and fire danger metrics (humidity, wind, precipitation, energy release component), fire size (hectares, >100 acres), suppression strategy indicators, impacts (threatened structures, injuries, fatalities), and regional/yearly fire counts. Descriptions appear consistent with a schema/metadata file.

llm_description: CSV table listing U.S. states and territories with total acres burned by wildfires and corresponding population. Columns include State, Total Acres Burned, and Population. Some jurisdictions have missing wildfire values (e.g., District of Columbia, Puerto Rico). Likely intended for comparing wildfire impact or normalizing burned area per capita across states. Data appears numeric and state-level; the time period and data source are not provided in the sample, so totals should be interpreted with that uncertainty.

CSV table of wildfire incidents with associated fire-weather summaries and incident outcomes for multiple U.S. regions, heavily representing California. Fields include start year, region/state, incident number/name, cause, dominant/subdominant suppression strategy, start and controlled dates, duration, average relative humidity, median wind, median ERC, rainfall total, threatened structures (primary/community/outbuildings), injuries, fatalities, and latitude/longitude, plus derived indicators (e.g., day-of-year, strategy indices, gt_100). Data spans 2002–2014 and 2016. The sample shows some inconsistent IDs/labels and mixed formatting, so minor parsing/quality issues are possible.

CSV table of wildfire incidents with associated fire-weather summaries and incident outcomes, primarily for U.S. regions (many records for California, plus Great Basin states). Fields include incident identifiers (region, state, incident number/name), cause and cause indicators, suppression strategy quartiles, start/controlled dates and duration, weather metrics (mean relative humidity, median wind, median ERC, rainfall sum), threatened structures (primary/commercial/outbuildings), injuries and fatalities, and location (latitude/longitude). Contains derived indicators (e.g., day-of-year, strategy indices, gt_100). Some rows show inconsistent formatting and mixed strategy labels, so minor data cleaning may be needed.

CSV table of wildfire incidents with associated fire-weather summaries and incident characteristics. Fields include start year, region and state, incident identifiers and names, cause, management strategy (dominant/subdominant), start and controlled dates, duration, threatened structures (primary/commercial/outbuildings), injuries and fatalities, and fire location (latitude/longitude). Weather/condition metrics include mean relative humidity (avrh_mean), median wind, median ERC, and precipitation sum (rain_sum), plus derived indicators (e.g., day-of-year, gt_100, cause/strategy indices). Sample shows mostly U.S. incidents, heavily from California; column meanings may vary slightly due to noisy/merged source formatting.

CSV table of annual Air Quality Index (AQI) statistics by county and state for year 2024. Each row represents a county (plus some non-state jurisdictions) with counts of days in AQI categories (Good through Hazardous), maximum AQI, 90th percentile AQI, median AQI, and days where each pollutant (CO, NO2, Ozone, PM2.5, PM10) was the dominant contributor. Includes total “Days with AQI,” which may be less than 365 due to monitoring coverage. Sample contains some irregular region names (e.g., Mexico/territories), so geographic scope may be broader than U.S. counties.

CSV table of annual U.S. wildfire activity and suppression spending from the National Interagency Fire Center (NIFC), covering 1985–2023. Columns include Year, number of Fires, Acres burned, Forest Service suppression costs, DOI Agencies suppression costs, and Total suppression costs. Monetary values are shown as dollar-formatted strings and counts/acreage include thousands separators. Useful for trend analysis of wildfire frequency, burned area, and federal suppression expenditures by agency and in total.

CSV table of annual acres burned by human-caused wildfires, reported by NIFC and grouped by U.S. geographic areas/regions (e.g., Alaska, Northwest, Northern/Southern California, Great Basin, Southwest, Rocky Mountains, Eastern and Southern Areas) plus a Total. Each row is a year (shown 2001–2024 in the sample) and each column is acres burned for that region. Values appear as comma-formatted numbers; at least one field (Western Great Basin) is blank in some years, suggesting missing data.

CSV of wildfire incidents (primarily U.S. western regions) combining NOAA-derived weather/fuel metrics and incident attributes. Fields include start year, region/state, incident identifiers and names, cause, strategy percentiles (dominant/subdominant), start/controlled dates and duration, humidity, wind, ERC, rainfall, threatened assets (primary/community/outbuildings), injuries and fatalities, and latitude/longitude plus derived day-of-year and indicator flags. Data spans multiple years (e.g., 2002+). Some rows show inconsistent formatting and mixed strategy labels (e.g., "Other"), so minor parsing/standardization issues may exist.

CSV table of annual wildfire totals from the National Interagency Fire Center (NIFC). Each record lists a Year, the number of Fires, and total Acres burned. The sample spans 1983–2024, with fires and acres reported as comma-formatted numbers; at least one value includes a leading asterisk (e.g., 2004 acres), which may indicate a note or data flag. Useful for trend analysis of U.S. wildfire frequency and burned area over time.

CSV table of monthly NOAA wildfire summary statistics. Each record includes a Date field formatted as YYYYMM and measures total Acres Burned, Number of Fires, and computed Acres Burned per Fire for that month. The sample spans 2000-01 through 2025-03, enabling trend and seasonality analysis of wildfire activity and average fire size over time. Values appear numeric and consistently structured, though the source methodology and geographic scope are not included in the file sample.

CSV table of Remote Automated Weather Station (RAWS) records from a public view export. Each row represents an observation for a named station, including observation time, station identifiers (e.g., WX ID, NESS ID, NWS ID, MesoWest IDs), location (latitude/longitude, state, county), elevation, agency/region/unit, and station status. Reported weather and fuel variables include rain accumulation, wind speed/direction (including peak), air temperature, fuel temperature, relative humidity, fuel moisture, solar radiation, and battery voltage, plus reference links to MesoWest/NOAA time series. Some fields contain “NO DATA” and placeholder dates, so completeness varies.

CSV table of annual wildfire activity totals from the National Interagency Fire Center (NIFC). Each record represents a year and includes the number of fires and total acres burned. Values are formatted with thousands separators and at least one year (2004) shows an asterisk-prefixed acres value, indicating a possible note or estimate that may require cleaning. Useful for trend analysis of wildfire frequency and burned area over time.

CSV table of U.S. wildfire incidents attributed to NOAA-related wildfire data, covering multiple regions and states (sample shows 2002–2016). Each row represents an incident with identifiers (year, region, incident number/name), location (latitude/longitude), timing (start and controlled dates, duration, day-of-year), weather/fuel metrics (AVRH mean, median wind, median ERC, rain sum), cause and encoded indicators, suppression/management strategies (dominant and subdominant), and outcome fields (threatened structures, injuries, fatalities). Some fields and codes appear inconsistent or noisy in the sample (e.g., mixed incident IDs/strategies), so exact definitions may require reference documentation.

CSV table of monthly NOAA wildfire summary statistics by month (YYYYMM). Columns include Date, total Acres Burned, Number of Fires, and computed Acres Burned per Fire. The sample spans 2000-01 through 2025-03, providing a time series suitable for trend analysis, seasonality, and year-over-year comparisons of wildfire activity and average fire size. Values appear numeric and consistently structured, with Date encoded as an integer month identifier rather than a full timestamp.

CSV table of wildfire incident records, primarily for the United States (many examples from California and the Great Basin). Each row represents an incident and includes start year, region/state, incident name/number, cause indicators, suppression strategy quantiles (25/50/75) and sub-dominant strategy, start and controlled dates, duration, and impacted counts (threatened structures, injuries, fatalities). It also contains summary weather and fuel metrics (e.g., wind median, ERC median, rainfall sum), station/verification fields, and latitude/longitude plus day-of-year fields. Some fields and codes appear inconsistent or noisy in the sample, so exact definitions may require verification.

CSV table of annual acres burned from human-caused wildfires, reported by NIFC by geographic area. Columns include Year and regional totals for Alaska, Northwest, Northern California, Southern California, Northern Rockies, Great Basin (with a separate Western Great Basin field), Southwest, Rocky Mountains, Eastern Area, Southern Area, and an overall Total. Years in the sample span 2001–2024. Values are numeric acre counts, often formatted with thousands separators, and some cells appear blank (e.g., Western Great Basin in many recent rows), so minor parsing/coverage uncertainty may exist.

CSV table of National Interagency Fire Center (NIFC) wildfire area burned (acres) attributed to lightning, summarized by year and geographic area. Columns include Alaska, Northwest, Northern California, Southern California, Northern Rockies, Great Basin, Western Great Basin*, Southwest, Rocky Mountains, Eastern Area, Southern Area, and a Total. Years shown in the sample span at least 2001–2024. Values are reported as comma-separated numbers (likely acres). The sample contains a possible corrupted year entry (e.g., “38” between 2019 and 2017) and some blank cells (Western Great Basin*), so minor data cleaning/validation may be needed.
